title: Covellia lanceolata
taxonomy: Covellia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of this plant places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Within the family Rubiaceae, Covellia lanceolata is further classified as belonging to the genus Covellia and the order Gentianales. At the species level, it is identified as Covellia lanceolata.

intro: Covellia lanceolata, also known as Lance-leaved Covellia, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the Americas. It has been traditionally used to alleviate various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Covellia lanceolata include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
